note: Must-include entries merged into public/locations.stpete.json by build-city (not in OSM, or curated). Committed source — edit here, not the generated file. Difficulty / inPlay / fameScore are assigned downstream by the fame pass (apply-difficulty.mjs), so they are intentionally omitted here.

locations.name: 3 Daughters Brewing
locations.clue: A large, popular craft brewery with a big taproom.

locations.name: Albert Whitted Park
locations.clue: A waterfront park by Albert Whitted Airport at the south end of the downtown waterfront.

locations.name: Azalea Park
locations.clue: A westside community park and recreation center near the Azalea neighborhood.

locations.name: Bartlett Park
locations.clue: A south-side neighborhood park with a historic pool and recreation center.

locations.name: Clam Bayou Nature Preserve
locations.clue: A coastal mangrove preserve and paddling spot on the southwest edge of the city.

locations.name: Craft Kafe
locations.clue: A dedicated gluten-free bakery and coffeehouse on Central Ave near 66th St — the original location.

locations.name: Craft Kafé DTSP
locations.clue: The downtown outpost of the gluten-free bakery and coffeehouse, at 200 Central.

locations.name: Crescent Lake Park
locations.clue: A large lake-centered park north of downtown with a walking loop, ballfields, and the old Huggins-Stengel field.

locations.name: Demens Landing Park
locations.clue: A downtown waterfront park and marina at the foot of 1st Ave S, named for the city’s railroad founder.

locations.name: Friendly Fisherman
locations.clue: The Hubbard family's casual waterfront seafood spot at John's Pass — cook-your-own-catch and fresh Gulf fish since 1978.

locations.name: Five Bucks Drinkery (DTSP)
locations.clue: A longtime downtown dive on the 200 block of Central — everything five bucks.

locations.name: Fossil Park
locations.clue: A north St. Pete neighborhood park and recreation center off Dr. MLK Jr. St N.

locations.name: Gizella Kopsick Palm Arboretum
locations.clue: A waterfront arboretum of palms and cycads next to North Shore Park.

locations.name: Good Night John Boy
locations.clue: A downtown dance bar/nightlife spot.

locations.name: Hubbard's Marina
locations.clue: A family-owned fishing charter and marina at John's Pass Village, in continuous operation since 1928 — one of Florida's oldest.

locations.name: Kahuna's
locations.clue: A Gandy-area bar in north St. Pete.

locations.name: Lake Maggiore
locations.clue: The city’s largest lake, on the south side next to Boyd Hill Nature Preserve.

locations.name: Lake Vista Park
locations.clue: A southside community park around a small lake, with a pool and rec center.

locations.name: Maximo Park
locations.clue: A waterfront park at the city’s southwest tip near the Skyway approach, with a boat ramp.

locations.name: Mi Carreta Restaurant and Bakery
locations.clue: Family-run Colombian and Latin American plates with a bakery counter of fresh pan and pastries (formerly La Carreta Bakery).

locations.name: Mirror Lake
locations.clue: A small lake just northwest of downtown that gives its name to the surrounding historic neighborhood and library.

locations.name: My Rich Uncle
locations.clue: A downtown cocktail lounge.

locations.name: North Shore Park Kickball Fields
locations.clue: The open rec/kickball fields at North Shore Park.

locations.name: North Straub Park
locations.clue: A downtown waterfront green along Bayshore Drive, just north of the museums.

locations.name: Pioneer Park
locations.clue: A small downtown park just west of the waterfront, near the Dalí Museum and Albert Whitted.

locations.name: Rec Dec
locations.clue: A Gandy-area sports bar and social athletic club with lighted volleyball courts, cornhole, and a kitchen.

locations.name: Round Lake
locations.clue: A small historic lake and surrounding park in the Round Lake neighborhood.

locations.name: Sawgrass Lake Park
locations.clue: A north St. Pete nature park with a boardwalk through one of the area’s largest maple swamps.

locations.name: Sculley's Waterfront Restaurant
locations.clue: A waterfront seafood spot on the John's Pass Village boardwalk in Madeira Beach, sourcing fresh Gulf catch.

locations.name: South Straub Park
locations.clue: A downtown waterfront green along Bayshore Drive, south of its sibling park.

locations.name: Tequila Daisy
locations.clue: A downtown cocktail bar on 1st Ave N pouring 700+ tequilas and mezcals.

locations.name: The Crafty Squirrel
locations.clue: A downtown Aussie-style bar with artisan pizza and shareable teapot cocktails.

locations.name: The Don CeSar
locations.clue: The 'Pink Palace' — a historic 1928 resort hotel on the Gulf beach.

locations.name: The Oyster Bar
locations.clue: A St. Pete seafood institution since 1999, now in the Grand Central District after leaving its longtime downtown home.

locations.name: Welcome to the Farm
locations.clue: A downtown bar on 1st Ave N.

locations.name: Where's Jubes
locations.clue: An Aussie brewpub and sports bar on the 200 block of Central Avenue.

locations.name: Williams Park
locations.clue: A historic downtown square — the city’s old central gathering park, ringed by churches and a bandshell.
